sync package - sync - Go Packages

pkg.go.dev/sync     2026-06-02T00:00:00.0000000
Map is like a Go map [any]any but is safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines without additional locking or coordination. Loads, stores, and deletes run in amortized constant time.

Go sync.Map: The Right Tool for the Right Job

victoriametrics.com/blog/go-sync-map/     2024-10-04T00:00:00.0000000
Go's sync.Map isn't a magic bullet for all concurrent map needs. It's got some good tricks up its sleeve, like handling reads without locking, but it's not always the best choice. This article dives into how sync.Map works under the hood, from its two-map system to the bottom line of expunged entries.

An In-Depth Guide for Using Go sync.Map with Code Sample

reliasoftware.com/blog/go-sync-map
Learn how to use sync.Map, a high-performance, thread-safe map type in Go, for concurrent scenarios. See the underlying structure, reading, writing, and deletion processes, and a simple example code.

Concurrency in Go with Maps: Using `sync.Map` - Sling Academy

www.slingacademy.com/article/concurrency-in-go-with-maps-using-sync-map/     2024-11-27T00:00:00.0000000
In Go, concurrency is a powerful feature that allows functions to run independently and interact through shared data. One challenging aspect of concurrency is safely handling shared data structures, particularly maps. In this article, we will explore how to use the `sync.Map` type to store and retrieve data concurrently in Go.

syncmap package - golang.org/x/sync/syncmap - Go Packages

pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/sync/syncmap     2026-02-23T00:00:00.0000000
Package syncmap provides a concurrent map implementation. This was the prototype for sync.Map which was added to the standard library's sync package in Go 1.9. https://golang.org/pkg/sync/#Map.

The Internals of sync.Map and Its Performance Comparison with map ...

medium.com/@okoanton/the-internals-of-sync-map-and-its-performance-comparison-with-map-rwmutex-e000e148600c     2024-08-10T00:00:00.0000000
This article is for those who want to understand when to use sync.Map and when a regular map with a mutex is sufficient.

Go 并发控制：sync.Map 详解 - 知乎

zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/22878835460
我们知道，Go 中的 map 类型是非并发安全的，所以 Go 就在 sync 包中提供了 map 的并发原语 sync.Map，允许并发操作，本文就带大家详细解读下 sync.Map 的原理。使用示例 sync.Map 提供了基础类型 map 的常用功能…

From `sync.Map` to Concurrent-Safe Awesomeness in Go

dev.to/jones_charles_ad50858dbc0/from-syncmap-to-concurrent-safe-awesomeness-in-go-5f6g     2025-06-10T00:00:00.0000000
Better, but locks are the duct tape of concurrency—functional but clunky. sync.RWMutex ups the game by letting reads run free while writes wait, but in a read-heavy app (think caches), even that feels slow. Cue sync.Map: born in Go 1.9 to ditch lock drama with lock-free reads and a slick design. It's like Go saying, "Hold my beer—I've got this." sync.Map 's Superpowers: Speed ...

Understanding sync.Map in Go - Shaun Yuan

www.shaunos.com/understanding-sync-map-in-go/
The sync.Map is a concurrent map implementation that allows for safe access and modification of its contents from multiple goroutines. It provides an efficient way of storing and retrieving values by key.
